__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus data source scraping the OTLP -> Prometheus stream that carries mdk's OTel metrics.

description: mdk governance view (ADR 093): every gate decision the GovernanceEngine makes, from mdk.governance.decisions{kind,effect,mode,tenant}. The rollout story lives here - while a gate is mode=warn (shadow), a would-be deny is recorded as effect=warn; that warn-rate per kind is the parity evidence that gates the warn->enforce flip. Once a gate is mode=enforce, effect=deny is a real block.

options.content:
## mdk - governance decisions (ADR 093)

**What this shows.** Every gate evaluation the `GovernanceEngine` makes, from `mdk.governance.decisions{kind,effect,mode,tenant}`. `kind` = the dimension (model / runtime / skill / cost / quota). `effect` = the resolved outcome. `mode` = the rollout posture.

**The rollout signal.** While a gate is `mode=warn` (shadow), a would-be deny is **downgraded to `effect=warn`** - recorded, never blocked. The **warn-rate per kind** is your parity evidence: it tells you what *would* be denied if you flipped that gate to `enforce`.

**How to use it.**

- `warn-rate ~0 for a kind` -> nothing trips that gate in practice; flipping it to `enforce` is low-risk.
- `warn-rate non-zero` -> inspect *which* tenants/decisions before flipping (you'd start blocking them). Pair with the `governance.*` audit log for the specific reasons.
- After flipping a gate to `mode=enforce`, its blocks appear as `effect=deny, mode=enforce` (the bottom-right panel) - real 4xx/PolicyViolationError.

**Note.** `effect=allow` is the common case and is intentionally not audited (logs stay signal-dense); it is still counted here so rates have a denominator.

panels.description: THE rollout panel: would-be denials while in shadow. In warn mode a gate's deny is recorded as effect=warn. A flat ~0 line for a kind means flipping it to enforce is low-risk. Source: mdk.governance.decisions{effect=warn, mode=warn}.
panels.title: Would-be-deny rate by kind, in shadow (warn mode) - the flip-readiness signal

panels.description: Decision mix across all gates: allow vs warn vs deny. The denominator context for the would-be-deny rate. Source: mdk.governance.decisions by effect.
panels.title: Decision mix by effect (allow / warn / deny)

panels.description: ENFORCED denials: real blocks (PolicyViolationError / 4xx), by kind. Non-zero only after a gate is flipped to mode=enforce. Source: mdk.governance.decisions{effect=deny, mode=enforce}.
panels.title: Enforced denials by kind (real blocks)

title: mdk - governance
